title: Non-actionable alert — fires but nobody knows what to do
code_snippet:
- alert: GoroutineCountHigh
  expr: go_goroutines > 1000
  for: 5m
  labels:
    severity: critical
  annotations:
    summary: 'High goroutine count on {{ $labels.instance }}'
expected_feedback: Critical: Alert fires when goroutine count exceeds 1000, but what should on-call do? No runbook, no diagnosis steps, no mitigation action. 'High goroutines' could be normal under load, a leak, or a deadlock — the alert doesn't distinguish. If on-call's only option is 'look at it and hope it resolves,' this should be a dashboard panel, not a page. Either: (1) add a runbook with diagnosis steps, (2) convert to a warning with dashboard link, or (3) replace with an SLI-based alert that reflects actual service degradation.
